description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a dark, marbled surface and a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top face. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, giving the block a distinctive, textured appearance. The sides of the block are smooth and solid, with no visible seams or joints, suggesting it is a single piece of material. The coloration is predominantly black with subtle gray and white speckling, reminiscent of polished granite or basalt. The block is thick and robust, likely intended to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. Its design suggests durability and resistance to weathering, making it suitable for outdoor use.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface and a dark, marbled appearance. The block is solid and robust, with a distinctive striped texture.
description_view_0: The block is seen from the grooved side, showing the shallow depth of the grooves and the marbled stone texture.
description_view_1: This view highlights the deep, parallel grooves running across the top, emphasizing the striped pattern.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, this side shows the grooved surface but from the opposite direction, with the marbled texture visible.
description_view_3: The grooves are again prominent, with the striped pattern clearly visible and the stone's coloration apparent.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or spaced as a standalone barrier
